original_image_tags: Aged, yellowed manila paper page, showing the reverse side of a document. Two punched holes are visible at the top edge. The page contains multiple overlapping, faded ink stamps indicating receipt by various offices of the U.S. Department of Justice and FBI. Faint reverse text from the obverse side is visible through the paper.

Faint, reversed text is visible through the paper from the opposite side, including what appears to be the words "SUBJECT: AIRCRAFT ACCIDENT - SIGHTING - UFO" and other typed content, but it is not legible in this view.

elements.description: The page contains no photographs, sketches, diagrams, maps, or charts. It features multiple stamped ink impressions on an aged piece of paper with faint bleed-through text from the reverse side. Visible stamps include: one blue stamp reading 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE F.B.I. REC'D ESPIONAGE SEP 9 4:23 PM '52'; one red stamp reading 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE F.B.I. REC'D ESPIONAGE AUG 25 11:08 AM '52'; one blue stamp reading 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE F.B.I. REC'D 2:29 PM '52'; and several other overlapping stamps with dates in August and September 1952, including text like 'RECEIVED-L.A.', 'REC'D BELMONT', 'DEPT. OF JUSTICE', and 'SOD'S OFFICE'. Two punched holes are visible at the top edge.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content (aged paper, ink stamps, punched holes, bleed-through text) and categories, but Description B adds specific quoted stamp text and dates that Description A does not provide, without contradiction.
